notes: pricing-svc (the deepest hop of the gRPC chain mobile-bff -> orders-api -> quote-svc -> pricing-svc) develops a slow dependency: commit c41a77e0 made GetPrice synchronously reload the pricing-rules-store cache on a miss, so GetPrice p99 climbs from ~45ms to >2s. quote-svc calls pricing-svc with a 2s deadline and starts returning DeadlineExceeded; orders-api then deadlines calling quote-svc; mobile-bff at the edge sees 5xx and is paged. pricing-svc itself logs only slow-query/cache-reload latency at WARN with status OK spans, so it is NOT the loudest or an ERROR. quote-svc is a STRONG decoy: it deployed v488 right at onset AND emits its own real ERROR logs (HTTP 422 config-validation rejecting a malformed promo rule) that are flat/benign and NOT on the deadline chain. The tempting wrong answer is quote-svc (errored + just shipped, mid-chain); the true origin is pricing-svc whose latency onset is earliest and whose name appears as the deadline target of quote-svc's timeouts. search-svc is a healthy control that calls quote-svc but stays green.
_design_note: MEDIUM: breaks the 'blame the loudest' heuristic AND the 'blame the service that errored + just deployed' heuristic. The deadline chain quote->pricing, orders->quote, mobile->orders points down to pricing-svc; quote-svc's 422 config errors are a separate benign fault.
